The spike-window section now drops the self-announcing "entries after 13:40 may be missing" string (index_status is just `"rebuilding"`), keeps `coverage_pct: 58` as the sole load-bearing meta signal, and reframes the entries as a genuine trap — they implicate `risk-scoring` but are all timestamped *before* the 14:05 spike, so the indexed slice is unrepresentative and an unhedged cause fingers the wrong service. This forces inference from two subtle signals (coverage_pct + entry clocks) rather than parroting a warning string, pushing toward the ~50% band while keeping the fork section, the 13:40 boundary, and the deterministic trace contract consistent.
